semantics: The VM-less interval setter atomically publishes a validated 1us to 60s interval; non-positive values restore the pinned 1000us default.
Every VM owns an independent running flag, start timestamp, schedule, dropped-sample count, and sample vector; no profile data is process-global.
Starting an already-started VM is a deliberate restart that clears the unconsumed session instead of merging timelines.
Sampling is cooperative at source statement checkpoints after the current location and always-on activation chain are published.
No timer or foreign thread reads a mutable interpreter frame, invokes JavaScript, or changes inspector, worker-checkpoint, exception, or pause behavior.
The Chrome output contains root and parent-sensitive call-tree nodes, samples, nonnegative timeDeltas, wall-clock microsecond bounds, hit counts, children, source positions, and positionTicks.
The Markdown output is derived from the identical sample set and contains hot self time, total-time call tree, caller/callee details, and file aggregation.
Anonymous, recursive, async, top-level, sibling-realm, idle, interval-change, restart, null-VM, and allocation-failure paths have bounded fixtures.
Home output slots receive owned StringImpl pointers while Bun slots receive owned by-value BunString wrappers; both release through Bun__WTFStringImpl__deref.
Stopping without requested outputs still consumes the session; repeated stop returns empty output slots and never republishes stale data.
